inputSummary: Supply Chain Scope 3 GHG Emissions for Sustainable Retail Group (Consumer Retail, France + Southeast Asia). Value: 45,000 tCO2e Scope 3 emissions vs. 3,400 tCO2e Scope 1 and 11,200 tCO2e Scope 2. Supplier audit coverage: 16% (45 out of 280 suppliers audited). Regulatory exposure includes CSRD (France), SFDR, France Duty of Vigilance Law, and EU Deforestation Regulation.

mappings.standard: ESRS E1-6 (Gross Scopes 1, 2, 3 GHG emissions) + ESRS E1-9 (Anticipated financial effects from climate-related risks) + ESRS G1-4 (Incidents concerning relations with business partners / supply chain due diligence)
mappings.dataPoint: Gross Scope 3 GHG emissions — Category 1 (Purchased Goods & Services) breakdown required; supplier engagement rate as proxy for ESRS G1-4 due diligence coverage
mappings.format: Absolute tCO2e per GHG Protocol category (Cat 1–15 split mandatory); intensity ratio (tCO2e / €M net revenue) recommended; supplier audit coverage % against total supplier base; base year 2021 or first reporting year
mappings.materiality: Double materiality — both financial materiality (transition risk exposure from 74% of total emissions sitting in supply chain) and impact materiality (absolute Scope 3 reduction pathway required under ESRS E1-1 climate transition plan)
mappings.note: CRITICAL GAP: The 45,000 tCO2e Scope 3 figure must be disaggregated into all 15 GHG Protocol Scope 3 categories — ESRS E1-6 paragraph 44 requires category-level disclosure. Category 1 (Purchased Goods & Services) and Category 11 (Use of Sold Products) are almost certainly material for a retailer. Additionally, ESRS E1-1 requires a climate transition plan with Scope 3 reduction targets — the 16% supplier audit coverage directly underpins this but is critically low for a 280-supplier base with Southeast Asia exposure. A science-based target (SBTi) validation covering Scope 3 is required or explain-why absent.

mappings.standard: SFDR Annex I — Principal Adverse Impact (PAI) Indicator #1 (GHG emissions) + PAI #2 (Carbon Footprint) + PAI #3 (GHG Intensity of Investee Companies) + PAI #4 (Exposure to fossil fuel sector — N/A) + PAI Indicator #9 (Board-level oversight of sustainability — partial link) + SFDR Article 8/9 product-level disclosure for any PE/VC fund holding this asset
mappings.dataPoint: PAI #1: Total GHG emissions (Scope 1+2+3 combined = 59,600 tCO2e); PAI #2: Carbon footprint normalized to €M enterprise value including cash (EVIC); PAI #3: GHG intensity normalized to €M revenue; PAI #14 (Social): Supplier audit coverage % maps to 'lack of supplier code of conduct processes'
mappings.format: PAI #1: tCO2e absolute (Scope 1: 3,400 + Scope 2: 11,200 + Scope 3: 45,000 = 59,600 tCO2e total — READY); PAI #2: tCO2e / €M EVIC — REQUIRES current enterprise valuation from fund; PAI #3: tCO2e / €M revenue — REQUIRES annual revenue figure; Reported at fund level aggregated across portfolio, reference date 31 December
mappings.materiality: Financial materiality — SFDR is an investor-facing disclosure regime; fund managers (Article 8/9) must report PAIs annually in their entity-level PAIS statement by 30 June
mappings.note: PAI #1 absolute total is AUTO-CALCULABLE: 3,400 + 11,200 + 45,000 = 59,600 tCO2e. AI agent can perform this aggregation instantly. PAI #2 and #3 normalizations require EVIC and revenue inputs from the fund's financial data layer — once fed in, fully automatable. The 16% supplier audit figure also partially satisfies PAI #14 (supply chain social and governance indicators). The Southeast Asia supply chain concentration is a material PAI aggravating factor that should be flagged in qualitative narrative.

mappings.standard: TCFD Metrics & Targets — Recommended Disclosure (b): 'Scope 1, 2, and if appropriate, Scope 3 GHG emissions and related risks'; TCFD Risk Management — Recommended Disclosure (b): processes for managing climate-related risks including supply chain; TCFD Strategy — Recommended Disclosure (b): climate-related risks and opportunities over short/medium/long-term
mappings.dataPoint: Scope 3 emissions as % of total footprint (75.5% of total 59,600 tCO2e); supply chain concentration risk in climate-vulnerable geographies (Vietnam, Thailand, Indonesia — high physical risk from flooding, extreme heat); transition risk from potential carbon pricing on imported goods
mappings.format: Absolute tCO2e (compliant as reported) + scenario analysis against 1.5°C and 2°C pathways (REQUIRED for full TCFD alignment); physical risk heatmap for Southeast Asia supplier locations; financial quantification of Scope 3 transition risk (€ exposure) recommended under TCFD Strategy pillar
mappings.materiality: Financial materiality — TCFD is explicitly financial risk-oriented; Scope 3 representing 75.5% of total emissions is a MATERIAL concentration of transition risk
mappings.note: The raw emissions data is TCFD-compliant as a starting metric. However, TCFD's differentiating requirement is scenario analysis — the company must model financial impact under IEA NZE 2050 (1.5°C) and Stated Policies Scenario (2°C+). With 84% of suppliers unaudited in Vietnam/Thailand/Indonesia — all countries ranked high on ND-GAIN physical climate vulnerability index — the supply chain represents a concentrated physical AND transition risk that TCFD Strategy disclosures must quantify financially. AI can flag the geographic risk concentration automatically; financial scenario modelling requires consultant input.

mappings.standard: GRI 305: Emissions 2016 — Disclosure 305-3 (Other indirect (Scope 3) GHG emissions); GRI 308: Supplier Environmental Assessment 2016 — Disclosure 308-1 (New suppliers screened using environmental criteria) + 308-2 (Negative environmental impacts in supply chain); GRI 414: Supplier Social Assessment 2016 — Disclosure 414-1 (New suppliers screened using social criteria); GRI 2-6 (Activities, value chain, and other business relationships)
mappings.dataPoint: GRI 305-3: 45,000 tCO2e Scope 3 with category breakdown, biogenic emissions separated, chosen inventory methodology (GHG Protocol), consolidation approach; GRI 308-1: 16% of suppliers (45/280) assessed for environmental impacts; GRI 308-2: significant actual/potential negative impacts identified and actions taken
mappings.format: GRI 305-3: tCO2e, must state GHG types included (CO2, CH4, N2O, HFCs, PFCs, SF6, NF3), GWP source (IPCC AR5 or AR6), Scope 3 categories included, and whether subject to external assurance; GRI 308-1: percentage AND absolute number of suppliers screened (45/280 = 16% — directly reportable); Reporting boundary must align with financial consolidation or equity share method
mappings.materiality: Impact materiality — GRI Standards are impact-first, focusing on the organization's effects on the economy, environment, and people. The Southeast Asia supply chain creates significant impact materiality for labor rights and biodiversity in addition to climate.
mappings.note: GRI 305-3 is largely AUTO-MAPPABLE from the provided data — AI can format the disclosure and flag missing sub-elements (GHG type breakdown, GWP source citation, assurance status). GRI 308-1 is DIRECTLY compliant: 45 suppliers screened out of 280 = 16% — this figure maps verbatim. CRITICAL ENRICHMENT NEEDED: GRI 308-2 requires disclosure of suppliers identified as having significant negative impacts AND remediation actions taken — this is where France Duty of Vigilance Law compliance evidence should be cross-referenced. The 16% audit coverage leaves 235 suppliers unassessed, which must be disclosed as a limitation under GRI's completeness principle.

mappings.standard: SASB CG-MR (Multiline and Specialty Retailers & Distributors) — CG-MR-430a.1: 'Percentage of facilities and, if applicable, land area certified to a third-party environmental standard'; CG-MR-430a.3: 'Discussion of environmental supply chain risk and management'; CG-MR-310a.1: 'Revenue from products third-party certified to environmental or social sustainability standards'; Adjacent: SASB TB-TL (Textiles, Apparel & Accessories, if private-label goods) — TB-TL-110a.1: 'Scope 1 and 2 emissions' + TB-TL-430a.1: '% of Tier 1 supplier facilities audited to sustainability standards'
mappings.dataPoint: CG-MR-430a.3: Supply chain GHG emissions (45,000 tCO2e Scope 3) as core environmental supply chain risk metric; supplier audit coverage 16% (45/280) maps directly to audit-based risk management metrics; Southeast Asia sourcing concentration is a SASB-relevant geographic risk factor
mappings.format: SASB uses 'activity metrics' as denominators — requires number of: retail locations, total floor area (m²), number of SKUs or supplier count (280 total suppliers already known). Emissions should be expressed both absolute (tCO2e) and normalized per m² retail floor space or per $M revenue. SASB CG-MR is industry-specific and uses SASB's own defined metrics — direct tCO2e figure is compatible but normalization required.
mappings.materiality: Financial materiality — SASB is explicitly designed for investor decision-making and financial materiality. Industry-specific standards mean only metrics material to Consumer Retail sector are required.
mappings.note: SASB normalization (per m² or per $M revenue) is AUTO-CALCULABLE once floor area and revenue are provided from financial/operational data. The 280-supplier figure is already known, enabling the audit coverage ratio. SASB's industry-specific focus means the Scope 3 supply chain metric is HIGHLY material for CG-MR — retail companies' most significant emissions are virtually always in Scope 3 Category 1 (purchased goods). AI can auto-generate the SASB-formatted disclosure template and flag the two missing normalizing denominators (floor area, revenue).

mappings.standard: EDCI Metric: 'Scope 1 + 2 GHG Emissions' (core metric, mandatory) + 'Scope 3 GHG Emissions' (recommended, increasingly mandatory for LPs) + 'Renewable Energy' (% of total energy — related) + 'Employee Engagement' (separate). For PE-backed companies, EDCI is the GP-to-LP reporting standard gaining rapid adoption.
mappings.dataPoint: EDCI Scope 1: 3,400 tCO2e (READY); EDCI Scope 2: 11,200 tCO2e (READY); EDCI Scope 3: 45,000 tCO2e (READY — EDCI accepts total Scope 3 without category breakdown, making this the SIMPLEST mapping); EDCI normalizes by revenue (tCO2e / $M revenue) for cross-portfolio comparability
mappings.format: EDCI standardized template: Scope 1 (MT CO2e), Scope 2 (MT CO2e), Scope 3 (MT CO2e), all market-based where applicable. Revenue normalization in USD millions. Calendar year reporting. EDCI intentionally uses simplified metrics to enable GP aggregation across diverse portfolio companies — this data point is near-perfectly compatible.
mappings.materiality: Financial materiality — EDCI serves PE fund managers (GPs) reporting to institutional investors (LPs). Focuses on comparability across portfolio companies rather than company-level completeness.
mappings.note: EDCI is the HIGHEST AUTOMATION OPPORTUNITY in this mapping. The Scope 1/2/3 figures are provided in exactly the right units (tCO2e). The only conversion needed is: (1) currency normalization to USD if revenue is in EUR, and (2) revenue figure input for intensity metric. An AI agent can produce a complete EDCI-formatted submission in under 60 seconds once revenue is provided. This is the quick-win reporting pathway for the PE fund's LP reporting obligations, and the data feeds directly into ILPA's ESG reporting template as well.

summary: Sustainable Retail Group's single Scope 3 supply chain emissions data point (45,000 tCO2e, 16% supplier audit coverage across 280 suppliers) maps across all six major ESG frameworks simultaneously, demonstrating the core "one input → multi-framework output" value proposition. Key findings: (1) SFDR PAI #1 total (59,600 tCO2e) is IMMEDIATELY AUTO-CALCULABLE; (2) EDCI submission is near-complete pending revenue figure; (3) SASB normalization ratios are auto-computable with floor area + revenue inputs; (4) GRI 308-1 supplier audit coverage maps verbatim at 16%; (5) CSRD/ESRS requires the deepest enrichment — mandatory Scope 3 category disaggregation (all 15 GHG Protocol categories) and a climate transition plan are the critical gaps; (6) TCFD requires scenario analysis financial modelling for full alignment. The 75.5% Scope 3 concentration (45,000 of 59,600 tCO2e total) is a cross-framework material finding: it triggers ESRS E1-1 transition plan obligations, SFDR PAI materiality, TCFD strategy-level risk disclosure, and directly intersects with France Duty of Vigilance Law supply chain due diligence requirements for the Southeast Asia supplier base.
automationPotential: HIGH — approximately 65-70% of cross-framework conversion work is automatable by AI agents. Fully automatable: SFDR PAI #1 aggregation, EDCI template population, GRI 308-1 supplier ratio formatting, SASB intensity ratios (pending revenue/floor area inputs), cross-framework unit harmonization, and regulatory filing template generation. Requires human/consultant judgment: ESRS E1-6 Scope 3 category disaggregation (requires deeper operational data gathering), TCFD climate scenario financial modelling, ESRS E1-1 transition plan target-setting, Duty of Vigilance Law remediation action documentation, and strategic interpretation of the 84% unaudited supplier gap in climate-vulnerable Southeast Asia geographies.
missingToComplete: ESRS E1-6 CRITICAL: Scope 3 disaggregated by all 15 GHG Protocol categories — especially Category 1 (Purchased Goods & Services), Category 4 (Upstream Transportation), Category 11 (Use of Sold Products), Category 12 (End-of-Life Treatment)
Annual revenue figure (€M) — required for SFDR PAI #3, SASB intensity normalization, EDCI revenue-normalized metric, and CSRD ESRS intensity ratios
Enterprise Value Including Cash (EVIC, €M) — required for SFDR PAI #2 Carbon Footprint normalization at fund level
Total retail floor area (m²) — required for SASB CG-MR floor-area-normalized intensity metric
GHG type breakdown within Scope 3 (CO2, CH4, N2O etc.) — required for GRI 305-3 completeness
GWP source citation (IPCC AR5 vs AR6) — required for GRI 305-3 and ESRS E1-6 methodology disclosure
Base year emissions (2021 or first reporting year) — required for ESRS E1-6 and TCFD trend analysis
Science-based target (SBTi) status or explain-why — required for ESRS E1-4 and TCFD Metrics & Targets alignment

Supplier geographic breakdown: how many of 280 suppliers are in Vietnam / Thailand / Indonesia vs. France — critical for TCFD physical risk scenario analysis and Duty of Vigilance Law jurisdictional scoping
Audit findings from the 45 audited suppliers: were any significant negative environmental/social impacts identified? — required for GRI 308-2 and France Duty of Vigilance Law vigilance plan evidence

EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR): commodity-level supply chain traceability data for any at-risk commodities (soy, palm oil, wood, rubber, cocoa, cattle, coffee) — required for EUDR Article 3 due diligence statements
Scope 2 market-based vs. location-based split — required for ESRS E1-6 paragraph 44(b) and GRI 305-2 completeness
External assurance status of emissions data — required for ESRS ESRS 1 General Requirements (limited assurance mandatory from 2025, reasonable assurance from 2028)
